_comment: Edit-style benchmark tasks. Each task asks the assistant to make a small, well-scoped, verifiable code change. The harness git-resets the kubernetes clone before each run so the two pipelines start from the same tree. Verification: after the run, we check (a) the right file was modified (touched_path), and (b) the new content contains the expected anchors (must_contain_after). False-positive resistance comes from anchor specificity — generic strings (`comment`, `field`) would let the wrong file pass; we use distinctive identifiers and short, exact phrases.

tasks.question: In the Kubernetes source under `pkg/apis/core/helper/qos/qos.go`, add a single-line comment immediately above the `GetPodQOS` function declaration that says: `// GetPodQOS classifies a pod into Guaranteed, Burstable, or BestEffort.`

must_contain_after: // GetPodQOS classifies a pod into Guaranteed, Burstable, or BestEffort.

tasks.question: In `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/defaultpreemption/default_preemption.go`, find the unexported helper named `isPreemptionAllowed` and rename it to `isVictimEligibleForPreemption` everywhere it is defined or called *within that single file*. Do not modify any other files.

In `pkg/kubelet/eviction/helpers.go`, add a single `klog.V(5).InfoS("rankMemoryPressure: ranking pods", "count", len(pods))` line as the very first statement inside the body of `rankMemoryPressure`. Do not modify any other function. Make sure the existing `k8s.io/klog/v2` import stays valid.

In `cmd/kube-controller-manager/app/controllermanager.go`, find the function `leaderElectAndRun` and add a doc comment immediately above its declaration. The comment must say: `// leaderElectAndRun runs the controller manager under a Lease-based leader election.` Replace any existing single-line doc comment on that declaration; do not add a duplicate.

must_contain_after: // leaderElectAndRun runs the controller manager under a Lease-based leader election.

tasks.question: In `pkg/registry/core/pod/storage/eviction.go`, add a small unexported helper function named `pdbAllowsEviction` immediately above `checkAndDecrement`. The helper takes a single `*policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget` argument and returns `b.Status.DisruptionsAllowed > 0`. Do not modify any other function in the file, and do not change any imports.
